tasks.author: tejas
tasks.title: Data → HTML bundler (build.mjs)
tasks.summary: Inlines hub.css, app.js, and the merged data island into one self-contained PM/index.html.
tasks.body:
The content pipeline. build.mjs reads the four PM/data/*.json files, flat-merges them into one #pm-data island, and inlines the engine CSS and JS into a single offline HTML file (ADR-2, ADR-4).

It escapes < in the data island so embedded markup can't close the script early ([[bug-02|BUG-2]]), and refuses to ship if app.js looks stubbed ([[bug-01|BUG-1]]).

comments.author: opus
comments.text: Build is deterministic — same data in, byte-identical hub out. That makes the CI staleness check trivial.

tasks.author: tejas
tasks.title: Calm command center hub (8 views)
tasks.summary: Dashboard, Board, Spec, Roadmap, Decisions, Debug, Docs, Activity — system fonts, zero deps, light + dark.
tasks.body: The reading surface: a single-page hub with eight views over one flat data island. Cool-neutral oklch palette, one blue accent, semantic status colors, system fonts only (ADR-6). Keyboard nav, ⌘K search, and an entity drawer that overlays any view.

tasks.author: opus
tasks.title: Schema + doctor validator
tasks.summary: Validates the data island and flags broken backlinks, unknown owners, and stale links before build.
tasks.body: A pre-build validation pass folded into build.mjs and exposed as rocketman doctor. Every cross-reference (backlinks, owner, epic, debug→task) must resolve to a known id, or the build warns (and fails in --strict).

comments.author: sonnet
comments.text: Doctor now resolves backlinks against tasks, ADRs, debug, and spec sections. PR up for review.

tasks.author: tejas
tasks.title: rocketman CLI (init · build · new · doctor)
tasks.summary: One binary scaffolds PM/ into any repo, regenerates the hub, creates entities, and validates links.
tasks.body: The front door. rocketman init copies the PM/ template + CLAUDE.md into a repo; build regenerates the hub; new task|adr|debug appends a correctly-shaped entity; doctor validates. Zero dependencies — pure Node.

tasks.author: tejas
tasks.title: /rm-ideate — forced product thinking
tasks.summary: Six forcing questions (demand, status quo, wedge, why-now) that must be answered before any code.
tasks.body: Stage one of the track. Refuses to let an idea proceed until it survives six questions about real demand and the narrowest wedge. Writes an idea brief that /rm-prd turns into a spec.

tasks.author: tejas
tasks.title: /rm-prd — generate the PRD
tasks.summary: Turns an idea brief into spec sections (vision, personas, requirements) written into PM/data/spec.json.
tasks.body: Stage two. Reads the idea brief and produces a structured PRD — vision, problem, personas + JTBD, user stories with acceptance criteria, non-functional budgets, non-goals, risks — straight into the spec data.

tasks.author: tejas
tasks.title: /rm-plan + /rm-build
tasks.summary: Plan lays the launch track into milestones + ready tasks; build allocates them to a sub-agent fleet.
tasks.body: Stages three and four. /rm-plan decomposes the PRD into epics, milestones, and a board of ready tasks. /rm-build turns the board into a work queue: it allocates ready tasks to sub-agents on demand, and — per the single-writer rule ([[adr-05|ADR-5]]) — is the sole writer back to PM/ data.

comments.author: sonnet
comments.text: Allocator picks ready tasks (deps satisfied, unassigned) and fans out. Parent serializes all writes back to the hub.

comments.author: tejas
comments.text: Make sure the activity feed records which agent touched which task — provenance is the point.

tasks.author: tejas
tasks.title: /rm-verify + /rm-test
tasks.summary: Verify runs the automated gate; test generates a crisp human test script and records pass/fail.
tasks.body: Stages five and six. /rm-verify runs the project's checks (build, lint, tests, the hub doctor) as a gate before review. /rm-test produces a short manual test script for a human and writes the result back onto the task.

tasks.author: tejas
tasks.title: /rm-launch + /rm-iterate
tasks.summary: Ship to production, then capture every post-launch change into the changelog and activity feed.
tasks.blockedReason: Waiting on the CLI ship hooks in RM-4
tasks.body: Stages seven and eight. /rm-launch ships (tag, changelog, release) and /rm-iterate captures modifications as new tasks + changelog entries. Parked until the [[rm-04|CLI]] exposes the ship hooks it calls.

comments.author: opus
comments.text: Stubbed the skill so it's ready the moment RM-4 lands the ship API.

activity.line: moved to Blocked — reason: CLI ship hooks

tasks.author: tejas
tasks.title: Diátaxis docs + README
tasks.summary: Tutorial, how-tos, reference, and explanation — plus the OSS landing README for launch.
tasks.body: The docs set, structured by Diátaxis and also rendered inside the hub's Docs view. Plus the landing README with a one-command quickstart, badges, and the MIT license for the public launch.

tasks.author: tejas
tasks.title: Fleet view — multi-agent runs
tasks.summary: A ninth view: a parent → sub-agent tree per run, so a fleet's work is skimmable at a glance.
tasks.body: Post-launch. Render orchestration runs as a tree: the parent agent, the sub-agents it allocated, the task each touched, status, and token spend — turning a fleet's work into something a human can skim. Builds on the provenance already in the data ([[adr-05|ADR-5]]).

tasks.author: tejas
tasks.title: Cross-repo rollup
tasks.summary: Aggregate many repos' PM/ data into one read-only org dashboard.
tasks.body: Post-launch. A read-only aggregator that reads multiple repos' PM/data and renders one org-wide hub — every in-flight task across every repo, in one place.

tasks.author: tejas
tasks.title: Inter-agent relay (agent bus)
tasks.summary: A conflict-free file bus in PM/comms so agents in separate terminals send messages and hand off tasks.
tasks.body: Multiple agents in separate terminals couldn't talk, so coordinating meant copy-pasting between them. The relay — PM/comms/ + the rocketman relay CLI + the /rm-relay skill — gives them a shared nervous system: presence, free-form messages, and explicit task handoffs (offer → accept → complete). Each agent writes only its own files, so no terminal stomps another ([[adr-08|ADR-8]]). Polled on an interval via /loop.

comments.author: opus
comments.text: Handed RM-8 off to the skills session over the bus — it accepted and is wiring /rm-verify now. Zero copy-paste.
